response.justification: While Sarah Smith is an exceptionally skilled full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices and 20 years of experience, she is fundamentally misaligned with this C++ and industrial automation-focused role. Her C++ expertise is significantly outdated (last substantial work over a decade ago), her recent 5+ years have been concentrated in TypeScript/Node.js ecosystems, and critically, she lacks any demonstrated background in industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or the industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, SDL) central to the position. The role would require substantial ramp-up in C++, domain knowledge, and security compliance rather than leveraging her existing expertise.
strengths: Exceptional full-stack capability and adaptability demonstrated across 20 years and multiple languages (TypeScript, Node.js, D Lang, Java)
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, and code quality mindset
Experience with modern cloud architecture (AWS), message-driven systems (through D Lang work), and distributed infrastructure (Kubernetes, microservices)
concerns: C++ expertise is significantly outdated with last substantial professional work over a decade ago at National Instruments; recent career focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and D Lang
Zero background in industrial automation, embedded systems, IoT, or automation machinery; experience spans web platforms, media, GIS, and financial technology
No demonstrated knowledge of critical security requirements including SDL practices, IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, or relevant industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, MQTT)

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ expertise with 10+ years since last substantial work
No industrial automation or embedded systems domain background
